request.question: Which network does the series created by a man born with the name Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible appear on?

title: Los Grutynos
Ben M. Baglio
Johnny Bravo
Van Partible
77 Sunset Strip
New Amsterdam (TV series)
List of Johnny Bravo episodes
List of GetBackers episodes
Odd Mom Out
Ali Saleem
sentences: Los Grutynos is an Argentine comic strip about a fictional family which come from the bottom of the sea of the City of Grutópolys, located in the "Bahía sin Fondo" (Bottomless Bay), San Matías Gulf, in Patagonia Argentina, created by Beto Noy, a business man born in the Río Negro province.
Ben M. Baglio (born 1960 in New York), created the brief for two series of children's books – "Dolphin Diaries" and "Animal Ark".
"Dolphin Diaries" features a girl and her family from Florida, who travel around the world as marine biologists and study dolphins.
"Animal Ark" features two children who work together to help animals and solve animal-related mysteries.
The books were written by commissioned writers in the UK under Baglio's instruction and published using the pseudonym Lucy Daniels in the UK.
Each author is named with a 'Special Thanks' on the copyright page – see Dolphin in the Deep copyright page for an example.
In the USA the books are published under the name of Ben M. Baglio so this name is effectively both a man born in 1960 and also the collective pseudonym for the group of writers who write the books.
Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.
The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.
He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.
Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.
Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".
77 Sunset Strip is an American television private detective series created by Roy Huggins and starring Efrem Zimbalist Jr., Roger Smith, and Edd Byrnes.
Each episode was one hour long.
New Amsterdam is an American television drama which aired for eight episodes in 2008 on Fox.

The series starred Nikolaj Coster-Waldau as "John Amsterdam" (real name Johann van der Zee), an immortal Dutch man born in 1607, who has lived in New York City on and off since he was 14 years old, and who is a homicide detective in the present day.
The series was nominated for an Emmy for Main Title Design.
"Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.
The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.
The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!
Show".
The episodes for the anime series "GetBackers" were produced by Studio Deen and based on the manga series of the same name written by Yuya Aoki and illustrated by Rando Ayamine.
The series premiered on Tokyo Broadcasting System in Japan on October 5, 2002 and ran for forty-nine episodes until September 20, 2003 under direction of Kazuhiro Furuhashi and Keitaro Motonaga.
The plot follows the "GetBackers", a group that retrieves anything that was lost.

The team is primary composed by Ban Mido, a man born with the illusionary technique "Evil Eye", and Ginji Amano the former leader of a gang called "The VOLTS", a powerful group in the dangerous territory called the Limitless Fortress in Shinjuku.
Odd Mom Out is an American comedy television series created by and starring Jill Kargman.
A 10-episode first season was ordered by the American cable television network Bravo.

The series focuses on Jill Kargman playing a fictionalized version of herself, Jill Weber, who is forced to navigate the wealthy mommy clique that resides in New York’s prestigious Upper East Side neighborhood.
The principal photography commenced in September 2014; the show is filmed at various locations in New York City.
The series premiered on June 8, 2015.
On September 22, 2016, the network renewed "Odd Mom Out" for a 10-episode third season shortly after the second season concluded.
Ali Saleem (Urdu: علی سلیم), best known by his alter-ego Begum Nawazish Ali, is a Pakistani television host, actor, scriptwriter and impressionist.
He became a contestant in Bigg Boss 4 in 2010.

He broke into the mainstream audiences through his impersonations of late prime minister Benazir Bhutto, and later playing the cross-dressing Begum Nawazish Ali on various television channels, including the Aaj TV network, Dawn News and Geo TV network.
His alter ego of Begum Nawazish Ali has now become his primary persona as he rarely appears as the male Ali Saleem.
He is a bisexual man born to a retired colonel father in Pakistan Army

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Which network does the series created by a man born with the name Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible appear on?
hop1_docs: Van Partible | Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".

Johnny Bravo | Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.  The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.  He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.  Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.

List of Johnny Bravo episodes | "Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.  The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.  The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!  Show".

Eddie Bravo Invitational | The Eddie Bravo Invitational (EBI) is an invitational 16-man tournament, featuring submission-only (non-striking) grappling.  Combat Jiu Jitsu, which does allow palm strikes, was added for EBI 11.  The event and its rule-set were founded by Eddie Bravo.

Efrem Zimbalist Jr. | Efrem Zimbalist Jr. (November 30, 1918 – May 2, 2014) was an American actor known for his starring roles in the television series "77 Sunset Strip" and "The F.B.I." He is also known as recurring character "Dandy Jim Buckley" in the series "Maverick" and as the voice behind the character Alfred Pennyworth in "" and associated spin-offs.  He also voiced Doctor Octopus in the 1990s "Spider-Man" animated series, and Justin Hammer from the second season of the 1994 "Iron Man" animated series.

Perou | Ralph John Perou (born May 1970), known professionally as Perou, is a British fashion, portrait and music photographer who has also appeared as a judge on "Make Me a Supermodel UK" and on season 2 of Bravo TV's American "Make Me a Supermodel".  He was part of the T.V series called "Dirty Sexy Things" on the British network E4 which aired in 2011.  Perou photographed models to be put in his exhibition at the end of the series.

Angie Dickinson | Angie Dickinson (born Angeline Brown, September 30, 1931) is an American actress.  She began her career on television, appearing in many anthology series during the 1950s, before landing her breakthrough role in 1956 in "Gun the Man Down" with James Arness and the 1959 Western film "Rio Bravo", for which she received the Golden Globe Award for New Star of the Year.
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Van Partible (Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible) and the animated series "Johnny Bravo"

- Van Partible — "born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971" and "best known for creating the animated television series 'Johnny Bravo'." (passage [1])
- "Johnny Bravo" — "'created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network'." (passage [2])
- "Johnny Bravo" — "'second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons'"; "'premiered July 14, 1997'"; "'ended officially August 27, 2004'." (passage [2], [3])
- Ambiguity/missing info: None; the network is explicitly named as "Cartoon Network" in the passages. No additional retrieval needed.
- Next-hop retrieval (priority): 1) Johnny Bravo — lede/infobox (verify network and broadcast details); 2) Van Partible — lede/infobox (verify creator birth name and credits).
hop2_docs: List of Johnny Bravo episodes | "Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.  The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.  The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!  Show".

Johnny Bravo | Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.  The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.  He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.  Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.

Bravo (New Zealand) | Bravo is the second New Zealand television channel owned and operated by MediaWorks New Zealand and NBCUniversal International Networks, broadcast via the state-owned Kordia transmission network and on MediaWorks' website ThreeNow.  The channel launched on 3 July 2016.  Bravo focuses on design, food, glamour and pop culture.

List of programs broadcast by Bravo | This is a list of programs broadcast by Bravo, an American cable and satellite television network owned by NBCUniversal that originated as a premium channel, when it launched in December 1980.  The channel largely relies on reality television, with some feature films and syndicated programming.

Live by Request: Johnny Mathis | Live by Request: Johnny Mathis is a live television concert by American pop singer Johnny Mathis that aired on May 28, 1998, on the A&E Network as part of its "Live by Request" series.  According to the television industry magazine "Broadcasting & Cable", the network "recorded its best ratings ever--a 2.3 rating/1.6 million homes according to Nielsen Media Research"—for the broadcast.

Mary Kay and Johnny | Mary Kay and Johnny is an American situation comedy starring real-life married couple Mary Kay and Johnny Stearns.  It was the first sitcom broadcast on a network television in the United States.  "Mary Kay and Johnny" initially aired live on the DuMont Television Network before moving to CBS and then NBC.

Cartoon Network: Block Party | Cartoon Network: Block Party is an arcade-style video game for Game Boy Advance developed by Majesco Entertainment and released in the United States on August 5, 2004 and in Europe and Oceania on September 15, 2004.  It features characters from Cartoon Network original animated series such as "Johnny Bravo", "Ed Edd n Eddy", "Courage the Cowardly Dog", and "Cow and Chicken".
